Stamp PDF Free: Add Custom Stamps to PDF Documents Online

Learn how to stamp PDF documents for free without uploading them to any server. Doc Signer lets you add custom stamps, logos, and images to any PDF directly in your browser.

What Does It Mean to Stamp a PDF?

Stamping a PDF means overlaying an image, text, or graphic onto a PDF document. This is commonly used to add company logos, official stamps, signatures, date stamps, or watermarks to documents. Unlike simply typing text, a stamp preserves the visual identity of your brand or signature exactly as you designed it.

PDF stamps are used across industries for approval workflows, document certification, brand consistency, and legal documentation. Until recently, adding stamps required expensive Adobe Acrobat subscriptions or complex software. Now, free tools like Doc Signer make this accessible to everyone.

No Upload Required

Doc Signer adds stamps to your PDF entirely in your browser. Your document never leaves your device, keeping your sensitive files private and secure at all times.

How to Stamp a PDF for Free

Step 1: Prepare Your Stamp Image

Create or gather the image you want to use as a stamp. This can be a company logo, official stamp, signature, or any PNG or JPG image. For best results, use an image with a transparent background (PNG format). The ideal size is between 300-600 pixels wide.

Step 2: Upload Your PDF

Open Doc Signer and upload the PDF you want to stamp. The tool supports single-page and multi-page PDFs. Simply click the upload area and select your file from your device.

Step 3: Add and Position Your Stamp

Upload your stamp image, then click anywhere on the PDF to place it. Use the handles to resize the stamp and rotate it to the desired angle. You can add multiple stamps and move them freely until they look perfect.

Step 4: Download Your Stamped PDF

Once you are satisfied with the placement, click the download button. Your stamped PDF will be saved to your device, ready to share or print.

Types of Stamps You Can Add to PDFs

There are many creative and practical uses for PDF stamping:

  • Company logos — Add brand identity to proposals, invoices, and contracts
  • Official stamps — Apply certified, approved, or reviewed stamps to documents
  • Signatures — Add digital versions of handwritten signatures
  • Date stamps — Include timestamp images for time-sensitive documents
  • Confidential markers — Add confidential, draft, or final labels
  • Custom watermarks — Overlay semi-transparent brand elements
  • Department stamps — Indicate which department or team approved the document

Who Uses PDF Stamping Tools?

PDF stamping serves professionals across many fields:

  • Accountants — Stamp financial statements and audit reports
  • Lawyers — Add verified stamps to legal filings and contracts
  • Real estate agents — Certify property documents and contracts
  • HR professionals — Stamp offer letters and employee documents
  • Architects — Add firm logos and review stamps to blueprints
  • Teachers — Stamp graded papers and signed permission forms
  • Small business owners — Create professional documents without expensive software

Background Removal for Cleaner Stamps

One challenge with stamp images is unwanted backgrounds. Doc Signer includes a built-in background removal feature that lets you remove the background color from your stamp image. Simply select the background color and tolerance level, and the tool automatically removes matching colors, leaving only your stamp graphic.

This is especially useful when you have a scanned signature or stamp on a colored background, or when you want a logo with a white background to appear transparent on the PDF.

Stamping Multi-Page PDFs

Doc Signer fully supports multi-page PDF documents. You can navigate through each page using the page controls, and place different stamps on different pages if needed. This is ideal for contracts with signature pages, certificates with stamps in specific locations, or reports that need branding on every page.

Frequently Asked Questions

How can I stamp a PDF file for free?
You can stamp PDF files for free using Doc Signer. Simply upload your PDF, upload your stamp image (PNG or JPG), click to place the stamp on any page, and download the result. No registration or fees required.
Can I add a company logo or custom stamp to a PDF?
Yes, Doc Signer allows you to add any image as a stamp, including company logos, custom stamps, signatures, initials, or any other graphic. The tool supports PNG and JPG formats.
Does stamping a PDF affect the original document quality?
No, Doc Signer preserves the original PDF quality. The stamp is rendered at high resolution and embedded cleanly into the PDF without degrading the underlying document.
Can I stamp multiple pages of a PDF at once?
Doc Signer lets you navigate through all pages of a multi-page PDF and place stamps individually on each page. You can use the same or different stamps on each page as needed.
Is my PDF uploaded to a server when stamping?
No, Doc Signer processes all files entirely in your browser. Your PDF never leaves your device, ensuring complete privacy and security throughout the stamping process.

Ready to Stamp Your PDF?

Start using Doc Signer to add custom stamps to your PDF documents. Free, fast, and your files stay private on your device.
